content: I'm so excited to start my new YouTube channel about drone photography. I've been reading a lot of books on the subject to get some inspiration.
That sounds like a great project. What drew you to the topic of drone photography?
I've always been fascinated by technology and photography. I've also been home brewing beer for a few years now, and I see a lot of similarities between the two in terms of attention to detail.
That's really interesting. I wouldn't have thought to compare drone photography and home brewing. Are you planning on featuring any notable locations in your YouTube channel?
Yeah, I'm actually planning to film some videos in the mountains near my home in Colorado. I love the scenery and the challenge of capturing it with my drone.
That sounds like a great idea. The mountains can be a really beautiful and dramatic backdrop for drone photography. What made you decide to become a cybersecurity consultant?

I've always been interested in technology and problem-solving, and I found that cybersecurity was a great way to combine those interests. I actually got my start in the field about 10 years ago, when I was working on a project with my colleague, Alex.
That's great. It's always good to have a strong foundation in the field. What do you like to do in your free time besides drone photography and home brewing?
I also enjoy speedcubing and playing chess. I find that it helps me relax and think strategically.
That makes sense. It's great that you have so many hobbies. Do you find that your work as a cybersecurity consultant gives you a lot of opportunities to learn new things?
Yeah, I do. I've been learning a lot about new technologies and techniques, including some programming languages like Python and Java.
That's really useful. It's always good to stay up-to-date with the latest developments in the field. Do you have any fun plans for the rest of the year?
Yeah, I'm actually planning a trip to California in August to attend a conference and learn more about the latest advancements in drone technology.
That sounds like a great opportunity. I'm sure you'll learn a lot and have some fun too. Good luck with your YouTube channel and your trip.
Thanks, I appreciate it. I'm just excited to share my passion with others and inspire people to learn more about drone photography and technology.
